content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me locate atom index 16 (C:16) in the image.

From the visualization, I can see C:16 is in the right portion of the molecule, labeled as "C:16". Looking at the bonds connected to C:16:

- C:16 is directly bonded to N:18 (nitrogen, blue sphere, to the left of C:16)
- C:16 is directly bonded to C:15 (carbon, gold sphere, above and to the left of C:16)
- C:16 is directly bonded to O:17 (oxygen, red sphere, below C:16)
- C:16 is directly bonded to H:20 (hydrogen, white sphere, to the right of C:16)

I can confirm these are all direct covalent bonds by observing the connecting lines between the atoms. C:16 has exactly four bonds, which is consistent with carbon's typical tetravalency.

The neighbor atoms of atom index 16 (C) are:
- Atom index 18: N (nitrogen)
- Atom index 15: C (carbon)
- Atom index 17: O (oxygen)
- Atom index 20: H (hydrogen)

From the file name "geomconf_4348.xyz", the geomconf_index is 4348.

Let me verify once more by checking if there are any other connections I might have missed. Looking at the structure, C:16 is clearly connected only to these four atoms.

Based on the visual evidence, the neighbor atoms of atom 16 (C) are:
- 15: C
- 17: O
- 18: N
- 20: H

And the geomconf_index is 4348.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 16, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
